text: Maya said the transition to Lincoln High School has been rough. Lincoln runs a block schedule and she keeps missing office hours, so I suggested she email her counselor before Friday's quiz.
notes: Full school name first, then bare 'Lincoln' as sentence subject — collides with the president/street name and must still be scrubbed on second mention.

text: Deshawn transferred to Central in October after the move. Central's counseling office still hasn't forwarded his IEP paperwork, and Dad wants us to nudge them this week.
notes: School referred to only as bare 'Central' — never matches the known form 'Central High School' by string equality; possessive 'Central's' adds a second surface form.

text: In her essay draft Anika writes about the gravel trail that runs along the riverside behind her house, and how joining cross country at Riverside East High finally gave her a team. Keep the trail image, scrub the rest.
notes: Lowercase common noun 'riverside' appears legitimately in the same text; only the capitalized school entity should be removed — over-scrubbing breaks the essay, under-scrubbing leaks the school.

text: Jonah started at Park High in August after two years of homeschooling. Park has a strong debate program and he wants to try out, but he's worried about the time commitment on top of his weekend job.
notes: Bare 'Park' as sentence subject on second mention — maximal common-noun collision.

text: From Priya's personal statement: "Franklin taught me that showing up is half the battle. The first day I walked into Franklin, I didn't know a single person in the building."
notes: School name inside quoted essay text reads like a person ('Franklin taught me') — NER will likely tag it PERSON, not ORG; either way it must be scrubbed.

text: Colton's climbing essay describes reaching the summit of Longs Peak with his dad. He wants to enter it for the Summit Ridge Academy writing prize before spring break, so we're editing Thursday.
notes: Lowercase 'summit' used as a common noun two sentences before the school name; generic landmark (Longs Peak) should survive scrubbing while the school must not.

text: Tessa's mom called about the schedule conflict: Valley View dismisses at 2:15 on Wednesdays, so tutoring has to start at 3. Valley also runs mandatory advisory on Thursdays, which kills that slot too.
notes: Two-word school name truncated to bare 'Valley' on second mention; neither surface form equals the known 'Valley View High School'.

text: Malik mentioned most of his teammates are from the Heights and carpool together; he's the only one bussing in from the east side, which is why he keeps missing the 7am lift.
notes: Definite-article nickname 'the Heights' — a neighborhood/school shorthand with no 'School' suffix and no match to the known full name.

text: Sofia toured Madison on Tuesday and loved the art wing. Her aunt in Madison, Wisconsin offered to host her for the summer intensive if she gets in, which changes the July plan.
notes: 'Madison' does double duty as a prospective school and a city in the same note — both are unknown entities (not the declared school/city), pure NER territory.

text: Ethan's schedule request: Liberty North offers AP Chem 1st period only. If he stays at Liberty through junior year we should front-load his SAT prep into the fall block.
notes: Compound name shortened to bare 'Liberty' — abstract-noun collision on re-mention.

text: Parent intake form, section 2 — Student: Harper Diaz. Home address: 2214 Harrow Lane, Dayton, OH 45420. Preferred contact window is after 6pm on weekdays.
notes: Structured form-field address; the ZIP alone is a quasi-identifier and must not survive even if the street line is caught.

text: Transcript excerpt — Rowan: "yeah just have the tutor come to ours, we're at forty-seven twelve Beacon Street, the blue house past the church, you can't miss it."
notes: Spoken address with the house number spelled out in words — defeats any numeric street-address regex; only NER/rules on 'Beacon Street' plus context can catch it.

text: Dropped Amara's calc packet at 88 Quarry Rd since she was out sick. Her mom said the porch lockbox is fine for future drop-offs, no need to ring.
notes: Terse coach-note address with abbreviated 'Rd' and no city/state/ZIP context.

text: The uploaded W-2 header reads: Leo Kaminski, 1035 W. Ellison Ave, Apt 4B, Tucson, AZ 85713. Flagging that the employer copy also shows his SSN — do not attach it anywhere.
notes: Address embedded in an uploaded tax-document header, with directional prefix and apartment line — multi-token address formats stress the regex layer.

text: Zoe said the study group meets at the house on Copperfield Court, usually the one with the basketball hoop, Fridays after practice. Worth confirming a parent is home.
notes: Street name with no house number — number-anchored address regexes miss it entirely.

text: Isaiah's family is mid-move: they're out of the place on 19 Sycamore Ln as of the 1st and renting on Delmar until the closing, so send any mail to the school office instead.
notes: Two addresses in one sentence: one conventional, one a bare street name ('Delmar') with no suffix at all.

text: Pickup note for Nadia: her bus stop is the corner of 5th and Pemberton, not the school loop. The driver needs the change before Monday or she's stranded again.
notes: Intersection format ('5th and Pemberton') — no street-type suffixes, no number.

text: Finn lives off Route 9 near the Hannaford, about twenty minutes from campus, so evening sessions need to wrap by 8 or he misses the last county bus.
notes: Highway number plus a retail landmark triangulate the home location; the grocery chain is an unknown org not present in known_entities.

text: Camila's dad accepted the offer, so the family is relocating to Plano, TX in June. She will finish the semester remotely from her grandmother's place in Laredo, which shifts our session times an hour.
notes: Two cities, one with abbreviated state, one bare; the grandmother's city is an unknown entity not in known_entities.

text: Gavin grew up in Erie, Pennsylvania and keeps writing about lake-effect winters — strong essay material, but he name-drops his old block, Whitcomb Ave, in the second paragraph.
notes: City+full-state combo plus a suffix-only street fragment buried in essay commentary.

text: Lucia is only looking at programs in the Boise area so she can stay close to her mom. Flagging that the intake sheet still lists Cedar Rapids, Iowa as her home base — needs updating.
notes: City wrapped in a metro-area phrase ('the Boise area') plus a stale-but-real city+state pair from a form.

text: Owen's records request went to the wrong district — there are two Springfields in play, and his transcript is stuck at the Springfield office until a parent calls to release it.
notes: Maximally ambiguous city name, including a pluralized form ('Springfields') that a token-exact matcher will not treat as the same entity.

text: Reminder: Yusuf's showcase is in Bloomington, MN — not the Indiana one. His club already booked flights into MSP, so the itinerary is locked.
notes: Event city is an unknown entity, and the airport code 'MSP' is a location identifier that no name/city dictionary will contain.

text: Marcus wrote that his whole world used to be four blocks of East Garfield Park before the scholarship. He worries Chicago reads as 'too urban' to admissions — worth unpacking in Thursday's session.
notes: Neighborhood-level granularity inside reflective essay prose; 'Park' suffix invites confusion with a literal park rather than a place identifier.

text: Ingrid's robotics team, the Northgate Steel Stallions, qualified for state. She wants the award on her activities list, but the team name pins her school in one search.
notes: School embedded inside a team name; the mascot half ('Steel Stallions') is independently identifying and must be scrubbed even if 'Northgate' is caught.

text: Trey is section leader for the Mill Creek Marching Colonels this fall — a huge time sink August through November, so the SAT plan has to work around competition weekends.
notes: Band name is 'school + mascot' fused; 'Mill Creek' also reads as a literal geographic feature to NER.

text: Daniela made the Ravenwood JV Raptors as a freshman, which almost never happens. Her coach there, Coach Stebbins, offered to write one of her recommendation letters.
notes: Team name identifies the school; the coach is an unknown third-party person not in known_entities.

text: Kai's photo essay is all Friday-night stadium shots captioned 'Go Warhawks'. The mascot alone will out the school to anyone local, so the doc needs a scrub pass before the portfolio review.
notes: Mascot-only reference with no school name anywhere in the text — requires knowing the mascot maps to the declared school.

text: Bree was elected VP of the Harrison Forensics Society and wants it listed. Note their winter invitational is hosted at Beckman Hall downtown, which is a 40-minute drive for her mom.
notes: Club name embeds the school; the venue is an unknown place entity that also looks like a person's name ('Beckman').

text: Omar's team — FIRST Robotics Team 4817, the Circuit Breakers out of Delmont High — took the design award. The team number is publicly searchable, so treat it exactly like the school name.
notes: A four-digit team number that publicly maps to one school — numeric PII that no name-based scrubber will flag, plus a mascot that is a common English phrase.

text: Pickup logistics: Elise stays for art club at BCHS until 4:30 on Tuesdays, and the Briar Creek front office wants visitors to sign in at door 3.
notes: Initialism plus a truncated partial name in the same note; neither string-matches the declared 'Briar Creek High School'.

text: Nora's mom says everyone just calls it St. B's, but the official transcript will read St. Bonaventure Preparatory — make sure the records request matches the long form.
notes: Colloquial contraction with internal punctuation ('St. B's') — tokenizers split it; must be scrubbed alongside the formal name.

text: From the NPHS Band Boosters newsletter Jaylen forwarded: rehearsals move to the annex through October. Keep the clipping out of his shared file until it's been scrubbed.
notes: All-caps abbreviation inside a forwarded third-party document, attached to a booster org rather than the school itself.

text: Sadie keeps calling it Mo-Town in session — took me three weeks to realize she means Morristown High, not the record label. Update the glossary on her profile.
notes: Student slang nickname for the school that collides with a famous cultural term; the hyphenated form appears nowhere in any gazetteer.

text: Diego's counselor cc'd the WRHS thread: WRHS policy bars external tutors on campus, so we're moving sessions to the branch library on Kessler after Thanksgiving.
notes: Abbreviation used twice, once adjectivally ('WRHS policy'), plus a bare street name with no suffix.

text: Aiden was gutted after they dropped the sectional final to Kettering Fairmont on a buzzer-beater. Channel it — the losing-gracefully angle could anchor his leadership essay.
notes: Rival school is a wholly unknown entity — no dictionary entry, pure NER recall test.

text: Transcript — Imani: "everyone at St. Ignatius acts like they already own the conference, but we beat them at home in front of their own scouts, so."
notes: Unknown rival school inside quoted teenage speech, with a saint's name that could be tagged as a person.

text: Quinn's Model UN team placed 2nd at the Carver Invitational, hosted this year at Danforth Country Day. Three schools in the results sheet aren't in our records at all.
notes: Event name and host school are both unknown entities; 'Country Day' lacks any high-school keyword an abbreviation rule would key on.

text: Remy still tutors sixth graders at his old middle school, Pine Hollow, every Thursday. Great service-hours story, but it geolocates the family to a single feeder district.
notes: Feeder middle school is not the declared school and reads like a nature area ('Pine Hollow') rather than an institution.

text: Mom asked, off the record, whether Vivian should transfer to Oakcrest Collegiate for the IB diploma. Do not put this in the shared plan until the family decides.
notes: Prospective school mentioned confidentially — unknown org, high-sensitivity context.

text: Tobias got into the Whetstone Young Writers Camp, three weeks in July on the Averill College campus. It overlaps the family reunion, so Dad wants a call before they pay the deposit.
notes: Named residential program plus host campus — together they place the student at a specific location for three specific weeks; both unknown entities.

text: Mercy leads worship at Cornerstone Fellowship on Sundays and won't take weekend sessions. Her youth pastor, Pastor Deke, is writing one of her recommendation letters.
notes: Religious org plus an unknown adult identified by a nickname-style single name ('Deke') — both absent from known_entities.

text: Silas picked up extra shifts at the Dairy Barn on Loomis Street — money is tight this quarter, so keep test-prep costs off the parent-call agenda unless they raise it first.
notes: Employer plus its street pins the student's after-school location; 'Dairy Barn' reads like a common noun phrase.

text: Wren logs her volunteer hours at St. Aldric Medical Center, pediatric wing. The badge photo she uploaded shows her full name and the hospital logo — replace it with a redacted copy.
notes: Unknown hospital org referenced from an uploaded ID-badge photo description.

text: Dante refs U10 games for the Millbrook Parks & Rec league every Saturday morning. Between that and church his weekends are gone, so homework blocks land Sunday night.
notes: Municipality leaks via a parks-and-rec league name; the town is not the student's declared city and 'Millbrook' doubles as a street-name-looking token.

text: Freya says the Academy posts grades on Fridays, and her mom checks the portal before Freya even gets home — that's the anxiety spike we keep seeing in Monday sessions.
notes: School reduced to the generic head noun 'the Academy' — scrubbing it requires linking a common noun to the declared school; leaving it plus other context identifies her.

text: Callum's teammates on Westbrook's varsity squad want him at captains' practice all summer, which collides head-on with the July intensive. Need a call with his dad this week.
notes: School name appears only in possessive form ('Westbrook's') fused to the next token.

text: Aisha's district, Fox Chapel–Harmar, redrew the bus routes again. She now has a 90-minute commute each way, which fully explains the late-night submission timestamps.
notes: District name with an en dash (not a hyphen) — byte-exact matching against a hyphenated dictionary form fails; also identifies the school by proxy.

text: The rec letter Beckett uploaded ends with the teacher's signature block: 'M. Okonjo, Science Dept, Halloran STEM Magnet, Norfolk, VA.' The whole block has to go before this is usable.
notes: PII concentrated in a third-party signature block inside an uploaded document: initialed unknown surname, school, and city+state on one line.

text: Juniper toured Wexford on Monday and Ridgeline on Wednesday. She liked Ridgeline's ceramics studio, but Wexford offered more aid and her mom is pushing Wexford hard.
notes: Two prospective schools, both unknown entities, each re-mentioned bare multiple times with no 'School'/'High' cue words anywhere.

text: Rosa's schedule is central to the whole plan: Central Catholic runs a rotating drop schedule, so her free periods shift every single week and Tuesday can't be a standing slot.
notes: Adjective 'central' appears in its ordinary sense in the same sentence as the school 'Central Catholic' — a case-sensitivity and span-boundary trap.

text: Nico's highlight reel is captioned #KestrelPride and the account is tagged @kestrelhoops — both trace straight back to Kestrel Valley High in about two clicks.
notes: School identity smuggled through a hashtag and a social handle — the mascot fragment 'kestrel' inside handles must be linked to the org.

text: Opal: "the bus drops me at the gas station on Grafton Pike and I walk the last half mile, that's why I'm always late to the Tuesday Zoom, it's not a motivation thing."
notes: Street with the uncommon suffix 'Pike' inside quoted first-person speech; landmark phrasing makes it read like scenery, not an address.

text: Hollis opened with ten minutes about the ice storm back home in Chattanooga before we got anywhere near the essay. Momentum is fragile — keep the next few sessions tight.
notes: Home city dropped in incidental small talk, syntactically buried in a weather aside.

text: Margot's twin goes to Dunmore Tech while she's at Sacred Heart of Hillsdale, so the family juggles two bell schedules — joint parent calls need a 30-minute buffer either side.
notes: Sibling's school is an unknown org; 'Tech' suffix reads like a company, and the known school embeds the city name.

text: The letter Ezra's dad uploaded is on Talmadge Union High School letterhead, signed by AP R. Cuthbert. It's a disciplinary record — restrict visibility to coach-only until reviewed.
notes: Third-party administrator surname behind an initial ('AP R. Cuthbert') — role abbreviation 'AP' obscures that a person name follows.

text: Bianca fences with the New Rochelle Fencing Club three nights a week. Honestly the club, not school, is her main community — the genome should reflect that weighting.
notes: A city name embedded inside a club name — scrubbing the org token-by-token still has to kill the place substring, and the club is not in known_entities.

text: Grady says CHS lets seniors leave after 5th period. Verify against the CHS handbook before we build the internship block into his spring plan — I don't trust hallway rules.
notes: Three-letter school initialism with enormous collision surface (hundreds of US schools abbreviate to CHS) — still identifying in combination with the profile.

text: Annalise's poem's refrain — "under the water tower on Dekker Hill Road, all of Larkspur sleeps" — is gorgeous and utterly geolocating. Suggest she keep the image and cut the proper names.
notes: Street and town inside quoted poetry — literary register suppresses NER confidence, and the town also appears inside the known school name.

text: Final logistics for Theo's showcase: vans leave the school lot at 6am, stop at 412 Corliss Ave to pick up two teammates, check in at Brandywine Heights High by 8, and get back to Marietta around 9pm.
notes: Itinerary mixing a third party's home address (unknown), an unknown host school, and the home city in a single run-on sentence.
